notes: NaN test_avg/mae_surf_p due to vol_loss=Infinity on test_geom_camber_cruise. Only 9/50 epochs completed (2.4x slower per epoch). No n_layers=5 baseline for comparison. Recommended: gradient clipping + NaN guard, rerun at n_layers=6.

hypothesis: Pressure-channel loss weighting (p_weight sweep 2.0/3.0/5.0) on Huber+surf_weight=30 baseline
notes: All p_weight values worse than current baseline (103.2182). Note: PR was written against older PR #827 baseline (109.57); even vs that older baseline, only p_weight=3.0 came close (+0.24%). At p_weight=5.0 convergence is visibly impaired. Student analysis: normalized targets + surf_weight=30 already focus gradients heavily on surface pressure; channel reweighting adds no value and hurts Ux/Uy. NaN on test_geom_camber_cruise is pre-existing bug. Dead-end closed.

hypothesis: Huber + grad_clip 1.0 on rebased compound (post-#808: bf16 + n_hidden=256 + n_head=8 + Huber + epochs=12). Single-variable: --grad_clip 1.0.
notes: Outstanding result, wins all 4 val + 4 test splits. Sent back for rebase due to mergeable=CONFLICTING after PR #882 merged. Will need to re-run on full compound w/ EMA decay=0.999 + epochs=12 to verify post-rebase. Note: ran with epochs=50 cosine T_max=50, only 18 epochs in budget — partial third variable beyond grad_clip alone.
